en: A PSALM OF DAVID. The LORD says to my Lord: "Sit at my right hand, until I make your enemies your footstool."
zh: 耶和華對我主說： “你坐在我的右邊， 等我使你的仇敵作你的腳凳。”

en: The LORD sends forth from Zion your mighty scepter. Rule in the midst of your enemies!
zh: 耶和華必使你從錫安伸出能力的權杖， 你要在你的仇敵中掌權。

en: Your people will offer themselves freely on the day of your power, in holy garments; from the womb of the morning, the dew of your youth will be yours.
zh: 在你征戰的日子，你的人民都樂意投身； 你的少年人以聖潔為裝飾， 好像清晨的甘露，到你那裡。

en: The LORD has sworn and will not change his mind, "You are a priest forever after the order of Melchizedek."
zh: 耶和華起了誓，就決不反悔； 他說：“你永遠作祭司， 是照著麥基洗德的體系。”

en: The Lord is at your right hand; he will shatter kings on the day of his wrath.
zh: 主在你右邊， 在他發怒的日子，必剿滅列王。

en: He will execute judgment among the nations, filling them with corpses; he will shatter chiefs over the wide earth.
zh: 他必在列國中施行審判， 屍首就遍滿各處； 他要剿滅地上各處的首領。

en: He will drink from the brook by the way; therefore he will lift up his head.
zh: 王要喝路旁的河水， 因此他必挺胸昂首。
